UX Research November 2019

• Shaped over 10 prototypes with key insights, impacting product design and innovation.
• Recommended 50+ video conferences to drive data-driven improvements for 15+ products; identified customer pain
points through analytics, boosting customer satisfaction by 25% and retention rates by 20%.
Spoken Language Understanding using Pretraining Technique | SSRN Research Publication October 2020
• Achieved a 15% performance enhancement for the SLU model by conducting intricate dataset analysis, refining preprocessing techniques, and optimizing feature selection for NLP tasks.
• Resolved data-related challenges to bolster model robustness, resulting in a 20% increase in implementation accuracy and ensuring the delivery of reliable insights.
• Engineered a streamlined speech recognition model that achieved an exceptional accuracy rate of 95% using pre-trained machine learning. Managed a dataset comprising 248 distinct phrases across 31 intents totaling 10 GB in size.

Your resume presents a strong background in data science and analytics, demonstrating both technical proficiency and a solid understanding of business applications. Below are a few suggestions for enhancing your resume's impact:

1. **Professional Summary:**
   Consider beginning with a professional summary or personal statement that succinctly highlights your core competencies, significant achievements, and the unique value you bring to prospective employers. This section should be tailored to the specific type of role you're targeting.

2. **Quantify Achievements:**
   Wherever possible, quantify your achievements. Numbers provide concrete evidence of your contributions and impact. For example, in your "Experience" section, you mention orchestrating the implementation of an energy resource tracking system, resulting in a projected 15% reduction in energy usage. This is an excellent use of quantification. 

3. **Clarify Your Role in Team Projects:**
   For projects listed in your resume, clarify your specific role and contributions, especially when mentioning outcomes achieved by the team. This helps recruiters understand your capabilities and the nature of your involvement.

4. **Skills Section:**
   Your "Skills" section is extensive and shows a broad technical competency. Group similar tools and technologies together to improve readability. For instance, separate your programming languages, analytics tools, and project management tools into subcategories.

5. **Experience Descriptions:**
   While your experience descriptions are quite detailed, ensure they are not too dense, which might make them harder to quickly scan through. Use bullet points effectively by starting each with a strong action verb and keeping them concise.

6. **Education Details:**
   Place more emphasis on your recent educational qualifications, particularly on projects and coursework relevant to the positions you are applying for. Given that your Masters of Science in Information System is your most recent academic achievement, consider moving it to the top of the education section.

7. **Customization:**
   Tailor your resume for each application. Highlight experience, skills, and projects most relevant to the job you are applying for. This could mean reordering bullet points or altering descriptions to better match job postings.

8. **Contact Information:**
   Ensure your contact information is up to date. Given privacy considerations, ensure you're comfortable with the information shared publicly (e.g., in an online portfolio or LinkedIn).

9. **Proofreading:**
   Thoroughly proofread your resume for grammatical errors or typos. Consider using tools like Grammarly or having a mentor review it.

10. **Design and Formatting:**
    Ensure your resume is visually appealing and easy to read. Stick to a professional font, use adequate white space, and consider using subtle color or design elements if it fits the industry you're targeting.

Overall, your resume is strong, demonstrating a notable blend of technical skills, academic achievements, and practical experience. Tailoring and emphasizing your unique strengths will make it even more compelling.

Spoken Language Understanding using Pre-training Technique
Spoken Language Understanding using Pre-training Technique
Aug 2019 - Oct 2020Aug 2019 - Oct 2020

 
Associated with University of Mumbai
Associated with University of Mumbai
Show project
Proposed a pre-training methodology for end-to-end SLU models and used datasets to show that our pre-training techniques improve performance for SLU training sets. The end-to-end SLU systems map speech directly to intent instead of taking the long, time-consuming way of speech-to-text and text-to-intent mapping. For high accuracy, a large amount of training data is needed. We will try to reduce the data requirements of the end-to-end SLU model. A new SLU dataset is introduced, which improves performance when the full dataset is used for training purposes and when only a small subset is used. We also make additions to the model where new phrases are learned by the model which is new and not trained to the model during the training phase.
Keywords: Viterbi, HMM, ASR, NLU, WER

Next Word Prediction
Next Word Prediction
Aug 2019 - Aug 2020Aug 2019 - Aug 2020

 
Associated with University of Mumbai
Associated with University of Mumbai
Language modeling, also known as Next Word Prediction, is a crucial task in Natural Language Processing (NLP). It involves predicting the next word in a sequence of words. A statistical language model is trained on raw text data to estimate the probability of the next word in a given sequence based on the words that were already present in the sequence. Language models are essential components in larger models for complex NLP problems like machine translation and speech recognition. They can also be used as standalone models to generate new sequences that have the same statistical characteristics as the source text.

Language models learn and make predictions one word at a time. During training, the network processes input sequences of words, one word at a time, to learn and make predictions for each sequence. During prediction, the process begins by seeding the network with one or a few words. Then, predicted words can be gathered and used as input for subsequent predictions to generate a new output sequence. Thus, each model requires splitting the source text into input and output sequences to enable the network to learn how to make accurate word predictions.

Your academic projects highlight a diverse skill set, from data analysis to strategy consulting and technology implementations. These projects demonstrate your expertise in a variety of areas including data science, project management, risk management, and software development. Here’s how you might structure and articulate these experiences on your resume or a professional platform:

### **Data Science and Analytics Projects**
- **Customer Churn Analysis** | BCG Data Science Job Simulation, Forage (Jan 2024): Applied advanced data analytics and visualization techniques using Python (Pandas, NumPy) to perform customer churn analysis for XYZ Analytics, delineating strategic investigative approaches and uncovering crucial client data insights.
- **Healthcare Cost Analysis** | Syracuse University School of Information Studies (Aug-Dec 2022): Led a team using R for Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) and data cleansing, developing machine learning models (Linear, K-SVM, R-Tree) with 70% accuracy to predict high healthcare costs, facilitating actionable healthcare cost reduction insights.

### **Strategy Consulting Project**
- **BCG Introduction to Strategy Consulting** | Forage (Jan 2024): Enhanced strategic thinking by challenging conventional assumptions and employing brainstorming strategies to ideate sales revenue growth for a fictional luxury clothing brand.

### **Management and Risk Projects**
- **Project Management Insights** | Syracuse University School of Information Studies (Aug-Dec 2022): Utilized MS Project to investigate real-world project management challenges, exploring both local and global issues impacting competitive enterprises.
- **Café Shop Risk Management Assessment** | Syracuse University School of Information Studies (May 2022): Quantified and prioritized operational and management risks for a café shop, devising strategies to mitigate high-impact risks effectively.

### **Technology Implementation Projects**
- **Cloud Services Consulting** | Syracuse University School of Information Studies (Aug-Dec 2021): Collaborated in a team to transition video streaming services to cloud servers (AWS S3, Amazon RDS), analyzing cost (TCOS $1,611,267.43/year) and proposing long-term backup methodologies.
- **Fingerprint-Based Door Unlock System** | University of Mumbai (Aug-Oct 2018): Developed a secure door unlocking mechanism employing Arduino UNO and a fingerprint scanner, highlighting the scanner's capacity to store and manage up to 200 fingerprints.

### **Software Development Project**
- **Food Management System Website** | University of Mumbai (Sep-Oct 2018): Engineered a comprehensive web platform using HTML, CSS, PHP, JavaScript, and MySQL to manage diverse aspects of a food management system, including menus, costs, and billing processes.

When presenting these projects, ensure you:
- Briefly explain the context or challenge addressed.
- Highlight your specific role and contributions if it was a team project.
- Showcase the technologies, tools, and methodologies employed.
- Quantify the impact of your work wherever possible (e.g., cost saved, accuracy improved, user experience enhanced).

This structured and focused approach will help prospective employers or collaborators easily understand the breadth of your experience, your technical capabilities, and your ability to apply knowledge to solve real-world problems.

now from the above information about my resume and projects, answer for me the below questions: Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

Hope you are doing well.

We have received your application for the open Applications Development Associate position.  Before moving forward with your application, we will need some additional information.  Please provide responses to the questions below.  We look forward to receiving your response. 

If offered the position with our company, are you able to provide written documentation showing you are authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship, either now or in the future?


What are your salary requirements?


When are you available to begin work?


Are you willing to travel/relocate to various unanticipated project worksites throughout the U.S.?


Do you have a bachelor’s degree or foreign equivalent in Computer Science, Computer Engineering or related field? If not, what is your highest level of education? 


Do you have at least two (2) years of post-baccalaureate experience as an Applications Development Associate or in any related positions?


Please describe your knowledge, experience, skill and proficiency with the following:


Java

ALM

SpringBoot

Octane

Postman

OracleDB

MongoDB

Kafka

PINXT

AKANA

Kubernetes.


Position: Applications Development Associate

Employer: TELUS International (U.S.) Corp.

Location: Las Vegas, NV

Country: United States of America

Work Style: On Site  

Description and Requirements
Job type: Full-time

Responsibilities:

Develop, modify, enhance, and implement software systems designed to support new or existing business initiatives. Perform maintenance and provide technical assistance and advice on existing software solutions. Work in most phases of a project which may require reviewing the work of less experienced staff members. Actively and consistently support all efforts to simplify and enhance the customer experience. Provide administration, application development, maintenance, and technical support by using good practice web programming techniques to configure robust solutions and integration designs. Code, test, and debug new software or make enhancements to existing software. Participate in code review sessions to validate adherence to development standards. Develop and maintain technical documentation. Maximize the efficiency of the various software systems by implementing, monitoring, testing and auditing new software programs. Position will be headquartered in Las Vegas, NV, with periodic travel/relocation required to various unanticipated project worksites throughout the United States.


Educ./experience:
Bachelor’s degree or foreign equivalent in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related field plus two (2) years’ post-baccalaureate experience as an Applications Development Associate or related role. Experience must include Java, ALM, SpringBoot, Octane, Postman, OracleDB, MongoDB, Kafka, PINXT, AKANA and Kubernetes. Position will be headquartered in Las Vegas, NV, with periodic travel/relocation required to various unanticipated project worksites throughout the United States.

Given the provided background and details on the position at TELUS International (U.S.) Corp., here's how Rahul Kotian could structure his responses:

---

Dear [Hiring Manager's Name],

Thank you for considering my application for the Applications Development Associate position at TELUS International (U.S.) Corp. I am excited about the opportunity to contribute to your team and would like to provide the requested information as below:

**Work Authorization:**
Yes, I can provide written documentation showing I am authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship, both now and in the future.

**Salary Requirements:**
I am seeking a competitive salary package that aligns with the responsibilities of the position and my qualifications. Based on my research, similar roles in the Las Vegas, NV area offer a range, and I expect something competitive within this range. However, I am open to discussing this further.

**Availability:**
I can begin work with [Company Name] upon completion of my academic commitments in May 2023. I am flexible regarding the exact start date and willing to accommodate the company's needs.

**Willingness to Travel/Relocate:**
Yes, I am willing to travel and/or relocate to various unanticipated project worksites throughout the U.S as required by the role.

**Education:**
I hold a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Engineering from Mumbai University and am completing my MS in Information System from Syracuse University School of Information Studies in May 2023.

**Experience:**
While I do not have two years of post-baccalaureate experience specifically as an Applications Development Associate, my academic projects and internships have furnished me with practical experience directly relatable to the role. For instance, my work on cloud services consulting and various data analytics projects demonstrate pertinent skills useful for application development and maintenance.

**Technical Proficiency:**
- **Java and SpringBoot:** Through academic projects and self-led initiatives, I have developed a foundational understanding of Java and SpringBoot, emphasizing coding best practices and software lifecycle management.
- **ALM, Octane, and Postman:** While my exposure to ALM, Octane, and Postman is limited, I am keen and fully capable of rapidly acquiring proficiency in these areas, backed by my quick learning curve in other technological tools throughout my academic tenure.
- **Databases (OracleDB, MongoDB):** I have hands-on experience with database management and operations, particularly with MongoDB, facilitated by project work during my studies.
- **Kafka, AKANA, and Kubernetes:** My knowledge in these areas is introductory. However, I am enthusiastic about deepening my understanding and application skills, being familiar with the concepts through self-study and coursework.
- **PINXT:** I have not had direct experience with PINXT, but I am eager to learn and adapt to new technologies and platforms.

I am genuinely excited about the opportunity to bring my unique strengths to TELUS International (U.S.) Corp., where I can contribute to innovative software solutions and the enhancement of the customer experience. I am particularly drawn to the role's hands-on aspect in developing, modifying, and implementing software systems supportive of new or existing business initiatives.

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the possibility of discussing my application further and am available at your convenience for an interview.

Best regards,

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

Aug 2019 - Oct 2020Aug 2019 - Oct 2020


Associated with University of Mumbai
Associated with University of Mumbai
Show project
Proposed a pre-training methodology for end-to-end SLU models and used datasets to show that our pre-training techniques improve performance for SLU training sets. The end-to-end SLU systems map speech directly to intent instead of taking the long, time-consuming way of speech-to-text and text-to-intent mapping. For high accuracy, a large amount of training data is needed. We will try to reduce the data requirements of the end-to-end SLU model. A new SLU dataset is introduced, which improves performance when the full dataset is used for training purposes and when only a small subset is used. We also make additions to the model where new phrases are learned by the model which is new and not trained to the model during the training phase.
Keywords: Viterbi, HMM, ASR, NLU, WER

Next Word Prediction
Next Word Prediction
Aug 2019 - Aug 2020Aug 2019 - Aug 2020


Associated with University of Mumbai
Associated with University of Mumbai
Language modeling, also known as Next Word Prediction, is a crucial task in Natural Language Processing (NLP). It involves predicting the next word in a sequence of words. A statistical language model is trained on raw text data to estimate the probability of the next word in a given sequence based on the words that were already present in the sequence. Language models are essential components in larger models for complex NLP problems like machine translation and speech recognition. They can also be used as standalone models to generate new sequences that have the same statistical characteristics as the source text.

Language models learn and make predictions one word at a time. During training, the network processes input sequences of words, one word at a time, to learn and make predictions for each sequence. During prediction, the process begins by seeding the network with one or a few words. Then, predicted words can be gathered and used as input for subsequent predictions to generate a new output sequence. Thus, each model requires splitting the source text into input and output sequences to enable the network to learn how to make accurate word predictions.
